A comma-separated list of destination do: A comma-separated list of destination domain names, IP addresses, or other network CIDRs to exclude from proxying. Preface a domain with '.' to include all subdomains of that domain. Use '*' to bypass proxy for all destinations. Note that if you scale up workers not included in networking.machineCIDR from the installation configuration, you must add them to this list to prevent connection issues.
A configuration policy creates configura: A configuration policy creates configuration objects on managed clusters.
A credential stores the access credentia: A credential stores the access credentials and configuration information for creating clusters.
A label expressions allow selection of c: A label expressions allow selection of clusters using cluster labels. A cluster must match all label selectors and label expressions to be selected.
A label selector allows selection of clu: A label selector allows selection of clusters using cluster labels. A cluster must match all label selectors and label expressions to be selected.
A list of resources that contain the key: A list of resources that contain the keyword "OpenShift" in any field.
A placement selects clusters from the cl: A placement selects clusters from the cluster sets which have bindings to the resource namespace.
A placement without any label selectors: A placement without any label selectors or label expressions will select all clusters.
A policy contains policy templates that: A policy contains  policy templates that create policies on managed clusters.
A policy generates reports and validates: A policy generates reports and validates cluster violations based on specified security standards, categories, and controls.
A project organizes all of your Google C: A project organizes all of your Google Cloud resources. A project consists of a set of users; a set of APIs; and billing, authentication, and monitoring settings for those APIs. So, for example, all of your Cloud Storage buckets and objects, along with user permissions for accessing them, reside in a project.
A proxy URL to use for creating HTTP con: A proxy URL to use for creating HTTP connections outside the cluster. The URL scheme must be HTTP.
A proxy URL to use for creating HTTPS co: A proxy URL to use for creating HTTPS connections outside the cluster. If this is not specified, then httpProxy is used for both HTTP and HTTPS connections.
A Red Hat OpenShift cluster that is host: A Red Hat OpenShift cluster that is hosted on the Red Hat OpenStack Platform in your on-premise data center.
A Red Hat OpenShift cluster that is runn: A Red Hat OpenShift cluster that is running in a Nutanix environment.
A Red Hat OpenShift cluster that is running in a vSphere environment in your on-premise data center.
A Red Hat OpenShift cluster that is running in your AWS subscription.
A Red Hat OpenShift cluster that is running in your Azure subscription.
A Red Hat OpenShift cluster that is running in your Google Cloud subscription.
A Red Hat OpenShift cluster that is running on available hosts from your on-premise inventory; bare metal or virtualized.
A Red Hat OpenShift cluster with its wor: A Red Hat OpenShift cluster with its worker nodes running on OpenShift Virtualization virtual machines.
A Red Hat OpenShift clusterpool that is: A Red Hat OpenShift clusterpool that is running in your AWS subscription.
A Red Hat OpenShift clusterpool that is running in your Azure subscription.
A Red Hat OpenShift clusterpool that is running in your Google Cloud subscription.
A saved search already exists for the cu: A saved search already exists for the current search criteria.
A saved search query is already using th: A saved search query is already using the name {{searchName}}. Please choose a different name.
A security control category represent sp: A security control category represent specific requirements for one or more standards. For example, a System and Information Integrity category might indicate that your policy contains a data transfer protocol to protect personal information, as required by the HIPAA and PCI standards.
A specific namespace on the cluster: A specific namespace on the cluster
A vCenter server root CA certificate tha: A vCenter server root CA certificate that, when added, reduces the number of web browser certificate warnings.

All DNS records must be subdomains of th: All DNS records must be subdomains of this base and include the cluster name. This cannot be changed after cluster installation.
All resources in this group have deploye: All resources in this group have deployed on the target clusters, although their status might not be successful.

An infrastructure environment manages yo: An infrastructure environment manages your hosts and creates clusters that share network, proxy, and location settings.

ApplicationSet application where Argo CD: ApplicationSet application where Argo CD application resources are created on the hub cluster. The hub cluster is responsible for reconciling and pushing the deployed application to the managed clusters.

ApplicationSet application where Argo CD application resources are distributed from the hub cluster to the managed clusters. Each managed cluster independently reconciles and deploys the application by using the received application resource.

Argo Server exposes an API and UI for wo: Argo Server exposes an API and UI for workflows. You can run this in either "hosted" or "local" mode.

Azure Resource Groups are logical collec: Azure Resource Groups are logical collections of virtual machines, storage accounts, virtual networks, web apps, databases, and/or database servers. You can group together related resources for an application and divide them into groups for production and non-production.
Changes to these settings might also aff: Changes to these settings might also affect other applications or subscriptions. This subscription deploys the following resources:
Channels point to repositories where Kub: Channels point to repositories where Kubernetes resources are stored, such as Git, Helm chart, or object storage repositories. Channels support multiple subscriptions from multiple targets.

Define a business application using open: Define a business application using open standards and deploy the applications using placement policies that are integrated into existing CI/CD pipelines and governance controls.
DelayAfterRunSeconds is the minimum seco: DelayAfterRunSeconds is the minimum seconds before an automation can be restarted on the same cluster. When a policy is violated, the automation runs one time before the delay period. If the policy is violated multiple times during the delay period and kept in the violated state, the automation runs one time after the delay period. The default is 0 seconds and is only applicable for the everyEvent mode.
Delete is not supported for managed clus: Delete is not supported for managed cluster resources in the global context. To delete this resource, please navigate to the details page on the managed hub.

Displays the desired number of clusters: Displays the desired number of clusters to which subscriptions that use this placement rule should be propagated.

Highly available means components should: Highly available means components should be resilient to problems across fault boundaries as defined by the component to which the policy is attached. This usually means running critical workloads with 3 replicas and with little or no toleration of disruption of the component.

operatorPolicy.startingCsv.labelHelper: If you want to install a particular version of your Operator, specify the startingCSV property.
operatorPolicy.subscription.labelHelper: This is the package name of the Operator to install, which might be different from the Display Name used in the catalog.
operatorpolicy.version.labelHelper: Versions is a list of non-empty strings that specify which installed versions are compliant when set to 'inform' mode, and which installPlans are approved when you set the parameter to 'enforce' mode.
Optional group name to associate volume: Optional group name to associate volume snapshot classes with storage classes
Optional: Enter a description for this s: Optional: Enter a description for this search query
Optional: Enter a group name matching a: Optional: Enter a group name matching a storage class group. This snapshot class only works with storage classes that share the same group name.
Optional: The base domain of your networ: Optional: The base domain of your network, which is used to create routes to your OpenShift Container Platform cluster components. It must contain the cluster name that you plan to create, and is configured in the DNS of your network as a Start Of Authority (SOA) record. You can also add this when you create the cluster.
Optional: The base domain of your provid: Optional: The base domain of your provider, which is used to create routes to your OpenShift Container Platform cluster components. It is configured in your cloud provider's DNS as a Start Of Authority (SOA) record.
Optional. Specifies how related objects: Optional. Specifies how related objects on the managed cluster are pruned when the policy is deleted. The parameter values are None, DeleteIfCreated, and DeleteAll.
Optional. Specifies the remediation of y: Optional. Specifies the remediation of your policy. The parameter values are enforce and inform. If specified, the spec.remediationAction value that is defined overrides the remediationAction parameter defined in the child policy, from the policy-templates section. For example, if spec.remediationAction value section is set to enforce, then the remediationAction in the policy-templates section is set to enforce during runtime. Important: Some policies might not support the enforce feature.
Optionally use an OpenShift Virtualizati: Optionally use an OpenShift Virtualization installation on an external infrastructure cluster.
Persistent volume storage class for etcd: Persistent volume storage class for etcd data volumes
Placement bindings can have multiple sub: Placement bindings can have multiple subjects which the placement is applied to.
Placement rules define the target cluste: Placement rules define the target clusters where subscriptions are delivered. This is done by cluster name, cluster resource annotation(s), or cluster resource label(s).
Placement rules determine which clusters: Placement rules determine which clusters a resources will be applied.
PlacementRule resource is deprecated and: PlacementRule resource is deprecated and will not receive updates or fixes.
Placements define the target clusters th: Placements define the target clusters that must subscribe to a ClusterSet where subscriptions and application sets are delivered. This is done by cluster name, cluster resource annotation(s), or cluster resource label(s).
Platform specific data for the add node: Platform specific data for the add node pool form will no longer automatically populate after the last node pool is removed.
Policy placement is managed by a policy: Policy placement is managed by a policy set.
Policy placement is managed by policy se: Policy placement is managed by policy sets.
policy.tableHeader.name: policy.tableHeader.name
Press space or enter to begin dragging,: Press space or enter to begin dragging, and use the arrow keys to navigate up or down. Press enter to confirm the drag, or any other key to cancel the drag operation.
Production environments can deny direct: Production environments can deny direct access to the Internet and instead have an HTTP or HTTPS proxy available. You can configure a new OpenShift Container Platform cluster to use a proxy by configuring the proxy settings.
Provide a kubeconfig that grants access: Provide a kubeconfig that grants access to an external infrastructure cluster running OpenShift Virtualization.
Provide customized control plane cluster: Provide customized control plane cluster configuration.
Provides a link to the resource reposito: Provides a link to the resource repository that is represented by the channel.
Provides links to each of the resource r: Provides links to each of the resource repositories used by the application.
Query error related to the search result: Query error related to the search results.
Quickly view service endpoints, pods, an: Quickly view service endpoints, pods, and dependant resources that comprise your application topology.
R5 - 5% additional memory per vCPU over: R5 - 5% additional memory per vCPU over R4
Red Hat Advanced Cluster Management for: Red Hat Advanced Cluster Management for Kubernetes provides the tools and capabilities to address various challenges with managing multiple clusters and consoles, distributed business applications, and inconsistent security controls across Kubernetes clusters that are deployed on-premises, or across public clouds.
Red Hat Insights gathers data and uses i: Red Hat Insights gathers data and uses it to produce actionable recommendations.
Reduces costs by efficiently reusing an: Reduces costs by efficiently reusing an OpenShift cluster to host multiple control planes.
Register a set of one or more managed cl: Register a set of one or more managed clusters to Red Hat OpenShift GitOps.
Related Kubernetes resources can be disp: Related Kubernetes resources can be displayed to help aid in the correlation of data from one object to another.
Remediation action will be determined by: Remediation action will be determined by what is set in the policy template definitions.
Removing {{name}} is irreversible. Selec: Removing {{name}} is irreversible. Select any associated resources that you want to delete in addition to {{name}}.
Replace resources instead of applying ch: Replace resources instead of applying changes from the source repository
Reports the violation, which requires ma: Reports the violation, which requires manual remediation.
Reports the violation, which requires manual remediation. This cannot be overridden by the parent policy's remediation action.
Request failed because of too many reque: Request failed because of too many requests. Please retry.
Resources from these managed hubs and ma: Resources from these managed hubs and managed clusters will be missing in search results. Resolve cluster issues to see all cluster resources.
Restricted networks which do not have di: Restricted networks which do not have direct access to the Internet require a mirror location of the Red Hat Enterprise Linux CoreOS image.
Return to a step to make changes: Return to a step to make changes
Run an OpenShift cluster where the contr: Run an OpenShift cluster where the control plane and data plane are coupled. The control plane is hosted by a dedicated group of physical or virtual nodes and the network stack is shared.

Run an OpenShift cluster where the control plane is decoupled from the data plane, and is treated like a multi-tenant workload on a hosting service cluster. The data plane is on a separate network domain that allows segmentation between management and workload traffic.
Saved search query limit has been reache: Saved search query limit has been reached. Please delete a saved search to save another.
Scope refers to the cluster associated t: Scope refers to the cluster associated to the PolicyReport.

Select a namespace to be able to select: Select a namespace to be able to select policies in that namespace.

Select an active cloud name for the cred: Select an active cloud name for the credential.
Select at least one cluster set to deplo: Select at least one cluster set to deploy application resources.
Select cluster sets from which to select: Select cluster sets from which to select clusters. If you do not select a cluster set, all clusters are selected from all cluster sets bound to the namespace.
Select clusters from the clusters in sel: Select clusters from the clusters in selected cluster sets using cluster claims status. For a cluster to be be selected, the cluster must match all label selectors, label expressions, and claim expressions.
Select clusters from the clusters in selected cluster sets using cluster labels. For a cluster to be be selected, the cluster must match all label selectors, label expressions, and claim expressions.
Select the new versions for the cluster: Select the new versions for the cluster and node pools that you want to upgrade. This action is irreversible.
Select to disable the policy from being: Select to disable the policy from being propagated to managed clusters.
Selected columns will appear in the tabl: Selected columns will appear in the table. Drag and drop the columns to reorder them.
Settings used to configure application s: Settings used to configure application syncing when there are differences between the desired state and the live cluster state.
Single replica means components are not: Single replica means components are not expected to be resilient to problems across most fault boundaries associated with high availability. This usually means running critical workloads with just 1 replica and with toleration of full disruption of the component.
Some policies have the Prune parameter s: Some policies have the Prune parameter set.
Some resources failed to deploy. Use Vie: Some resources failed to deploy. Use View resource YAML link to view the details.

Standalone clusters will display the nam: Standalone clusters will display the namespace used by the ManagedCluster resource. Hosted clusters will display the hosting namespace when the status is "Pending import" and the ManagedCluster namespace when the status is "Ready".

The ArgoCD pull model does not support t: The ArgoCD pull model does not support the hub cluster as a destination cluster. Filter out the hub cluster from the placement resource.
The authentication method to use to conn: The authentication method to use to connect to OpenShift Cluster Manager.
The automation template cannot be update: The automation template cannot be updated for any of the selected clusters.
The channel cannot be changed for any of: The channel cannot be changed for any of the selected clusters.
The cluster name is already used by anot: The cluster name is already used by another cluster.
The cluster pool deletion will be blocke: The cluster pool deletion will be blocked until all claimed cluster(s) are deleted.
The cluster pool will not generate machi: The cluster pool will not generate machine pools if the count is not equal to 3. Node scaling will be unavailable.
The contents of the certificate file tha: The contents of the certificate file that you used for your mirror registry, which can be an existing, trusted certificate authority or the self-signed certificate that you generated for the mirror registry.
The existing namespace where the credent: The existing namespace where the credential secret will be stored.
The following Argo application(s) deploy: The following Argo application(s) deployed by the application set will also be deleted:
The following disk will not be included: The following disk will not be included in the snapshot
The following disk will not be included in the snapshot
The fully-qualified host name or IP addr: The fully-qualified host name or IP address of the vCenter server. The value must be defined in the vCenter server root CA certificate.
The Globalnet CIDR to be used for the ma: The Globalnet CIDR to be used for the managed cluster (If left blank, a CIDR will be allocated from clusterset pool).
The Globalnet CIDR to be used within the: The Globalnet CIDR to be used within the clusterset pool. The default is 242.0.0.0/8
The health status for application {{0}}: The health status for application {{0}} is {{1}}. Use the Launch Argo editor action above to view the application details.
The health status for application {{0}} is {{1}}. Use the Launch Argo editor action below to view the application details.
The imageContentSources values that were: The imageContentSources values that were generated during mirror registry creation.
The impact of the problem would be {{tot: The impact of the problem would be {{totalRisk}} if it occurred
The instance profile for the node pool.
The instance type for the node pool.
The location of the Red Hat Enterprise L: The location of the Red Hat Enterprise Linux CoreOS image in your local registry.
The location of the resources on the Git: The location of the resources on the Git repository.
The maximum number of non-compliant clus: The maximum number of non-compliant cluster policy details that pass to the Ansible platform as extra variables. When it is set to 0, it means no limit. The default value is 1000.
The name for the node pool.
The name of the cloud section of the clo: The name of the cloud section of the clouds.yaml to use for establishing communication to the OpenStack server.
The name of the default vSphere datastor: The name of the default vSphere datastore to use.
The name of the infrastructure cluster s: The name of the infrastructure cluster storage class that is exposed in the guest cluster. You cannot change the mapping after creation.
The name of the infrastructure cluster v: The name of the infrastructure cluster volume snapshot class that is exposed in the guest cluster.
The name of the placement rule should ma: The name of the placement rule should match the placement rule name in a placement binding so that it is bound to a policy or policy set. The placement rule name must be unique to the namespace.
The name of the placement should match t: The name of the placement should match the placement name in a placement binding so that it is bound to a policy or policy set. The placement name must be unique to the namespace.
The name of the security control that is: The name of the security control that is being checked. For example, the certificate policy controller.
The name of the user that is required to: The name of the user that is required to access the vCenter server. This user must have at least the roles and privileges that are required for static or dynamic persistent volume provisioning in vSphere.
The name of the vSphere cluster to use.
The name of the vSphere datacenter to us: The name of the vSphere datacenter to use.
The name or names of security standards: The name or names of security standards the policy is related to. For example, National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) and Payment Card Industry (PCI).
The name that is used for this storage c: The name that is used for this storage class within the guest cluster.
The name that is used for this volume sn: The name that is used for this volume snapshot class within the guest cluster.
The namespace on the hub cluster where t: The namespace on the hub cluster where the policy resources will be created.
The OpenShift Gitops Operator is require: The OpenShift Gitops Operator is required on the hub cluster as well as all managed clusters you are targeting to create an application set pull model type.
The OpenShift GitOps Operator is require: The OpenShift GitOps Operator is required on the managed clusters to create an application set pull model type. Make sure the operator is installed on all managed clusters you are targeting.
The OpenShift Gitops operator is require: The OpenShift Gitops operator is required on the managed clusters to have an application set pull model type. Make sure the operator is installed on all managed clusters you are targeting.
The OpenStack clouds.yaml file, includin: The OpenStack clouds.yaml file, including the password, to connect to the OpenStack server.
The OpenStack instance type of the gatew: The OpenStack instance type of the gateway node that will be created on the managed cluster (default PnTAE.CPU_4_Memory_8192_Disk_50).
The operator is installed in this namesp: The operator is installed in this namespace.
The password associated with the vCenter: The password associated with the vCenter username.
The placement binding name must be uniqu: The placement binding name must be unique to the namespace.
The placement name should match the name: The placement name should match the name of a placement in this namespace..
The placement referenced in the Applicat: The placement referenced in the ApplicationSet is not found. Make sure the placement is configured properly.
The placement rule name should match the: The placement rule name should match the name of a placement rule in this namespace.
The placement rule used to select cluste: The placement rule used to select clusters for placement.
The private SSH key to use to access you: The private SSH key to use to access your cluster machines.
The provided cluster sets are not valid.
The provided namespace is not a valid Ar: The provided namespace is not a valid Argo Server namespace.
The public SSH key to use to access your: The public SSH key to use to access your cluster machines.
The pull secret that you obtained from t: The pull secret that you obtained from the pull secret page on the Red Hat OpenShift Cluster Manager site. Use this pull secret to authenticate with the services that are provided by the included authorities, including Quay.io, which serves the container images for OpenShift Container Platform components.

The subnet ID for the node pool.
The topology provides a visual represent: The topology provides a visual representation of all the applications and resources within a project, their build status, and the components and services associated with them.

The URL path for the Helm repository.
The version or versions for the deployab: The version or versions for the deployable. You can use a range of versions in the form >1.0, or <3.0.

This subscription deploys the following: This subscription deploys the following resources, which will be removed:
This subscription has no status. If the: This subscription has no status. If the status does not change to {{0}} after waiting for initial creation, verify that the application-manager pod is running on the remote cluster.
This subscription has no status. If the status does not change to {{0}} after waiting for initial creation, verify that the multicluster-operators-hub-subscription pod is running on hub
This subscription was not added to a man: This subscription was not added to a managed cluster. If this status does not change after waiting for initial creation, ensure the Placement Rule resource is valid and exists in the {{0}} namespace and that the application-manager pod runs on the managed clusters.
This value contains an HTTP or HTTPS URL: This value contains an HTTP or HTTPS URL to the image to use for Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform cluster machines. Optionally the URL can include a SHA-256 checksum. The value can also be the name of an existing Glance image.
This value provides one or more addition: This value provides one or more additional CA certificates that are required for proxying HTTPS connections.
This value provides the contents of the: This value provides the contents of the certificate file that is required to access the mirror registry.
To access a single cluster you can selec: To access a single cluster you can select it from the cluster list table.
To add a policy to a policy set, they bo: To add a policy to a policy set, they both must be in the same namespace.  There are no policy sets in "{{0}}" namespace.
To add policies to a policy set, the nam: To add policies to a policy set, the namespaces must match.
To apply a resource to a cluster, the pl: To apply a resource to a cluster, the placement must be bound to the resource using a placement binding.
To complete these values, use the imageC: To complete these values, use the imageContentSources that you recorded during mirror registry creation.
To create an infrastructure environment,: To create an infrastructure environment, you must configure the host inventory settings.
To get started, create an infrastructure: To get started, create an infrastructure environment to define the settings across multiple hosts.
To run automation manually, select \"Dis: To run automation manually, select "Disabled" and check the "Manual run" checkbox.)
To search for a keyword, type the word i: To search for a keyword, type the word in the search box.
To search for resources with a given pro: To search for resources with a given property value, type or select the property name from the autocomplete list. Then type or select the value for the selected property filter.
To view managed virtual machines, you mu: To view managed virtual machines, you must enable Search for Red Hat Advanced Cluster Management.

Use cluster labels and application place: Use cluster labels and application placement rules to easily move workloads across clusters, even between multiple cloud providers.
Use keywords or property filters to sear: Use keywords or property filters to search for resources.
Use labels to organize and place applica: Use labels to organize and place application subscriptions and policies on this cluster. The placement of resources are controlled by label selectors. If your cluster has the labels that match the resource placement’s label selector, the resource will be installed on your cluster after creation.
Use Placement resources to select cluste: Use Placement resources to select clusters from the cluster sets that you have bound to the resource namespace. An empty Placement returns all available clusters from all bound cluster sets.
Use policies to automatically configure: Use policies to automatically configure and maintain consistency of security controls required by industry or other corporate standards. Prevent unintentional or malicious configuration drift that might expose unwanted and unnecessary threat vectors.
Use search to query your resources. When: Use search to query your resources. When you save a search query, this view will show your data.
Use the Federal Information Processing S: Use the Federal Information Processing Standards (FIPS) modules provided with Red Hat Enterprise Linux CoreOS instead of the default Kubernetes cryptography suite.
Use the following command to get a list: Use the following command to get a list of available parameters:
Use the following command to get a list of available parameters:
Use the navigation to view the resources: Use the navigation to view the resources that are deployed by the selected subscription.
Use the rules filter to search for Polic: Use the rules filter to search for PolicyReports that contain a specific rule.
View all placement rules in {{0}} namesp: View all placement rules in {{0}} namespace
View clusters with search add-on disable: View clusters with search add-on disabled.
View system alerts, critical application: View system alerts, critical application metrics, and overall system health. Search, identify, and resolve issues that are impacting distributed workloads using an operational dashboard designed for Site Reliability Engineers (SREs).
VirtualMachine is either running or dele: VirtualMachine is either running or delete protected and cannot be deleted. To enable deletion, stop the VirtualMachine if running, or go to VirtualMachine details and disable deletion protection.

You can apply multiple values to the sam: You can apply multiple values to the same property filter. First, type or select the filter name, then select the value. Both values combine into a single filter.
You can include an operator (<=, >=, !=,: You can include an operator (<=, >=, !=, !, =, <, >) before a value selection to enhance your search. The search: name:!resourceName, would return all resources not named resourceName.
You can include any combination of filte: You can include any combination of filters and keywords to make your search more specific.

You use access keys to sign programmatic: You use access keys to sign programmatic requests that you make to AWS. The access key is equivalent to a username in a username/password combination.

Your client ID. This value is generated: Your client ID. This value is generated as the 'appId' property when you create a service principal with the command: 'az ad sp create-for-rbac --role Contributor --name <service_principal> --scopes <list_of_scopes>'.
